title: crotalaria lanceolata subsp. contigua
intro: Crotalaria lanceolata subsp. contigua, a type of flowering plant, is a species of the Crotalaria genus native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Its leaves, roots, and stems contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often in combination with other herbs, to harness its medicinal properties.
